title: Higher Education vs Vocational Training: Choosing Your Path
passage:
Two Ways to Learn After High School

After finishing high school, young people must choose what to do next. Many students want to continue learning. They have two main choices: they can go to university or they can choose vocational training.

University is often called higher education. It usually takes three to four years to complete. At university, students spend a lot of time in classrooms. They listen to lectures, read many books, and write long essays. They study subjects like history, science, business, or languages. People go to university because they want to get a college degree. This degree is important for jobs like teachers, doctors, lawyers, or office managers. University can be expensive, but many people think it is a good investment for the future.

Vocational training is different because it focuses on job skills. It is also called vocational education or trade school. This training is much shorter than university. It usually takes only one or two years. At a vocational school, students do not just read books. Instead, they learn by doing. They practice real work with their hands in workshops. For example, they learn how to fix cars, cook food, build houses, or repair computers.

Students at vocational schools want to get a job quickly. They do not want to spend four years studying theory. Vocational training is often cheaper than university, and students can start earning money sooner.

Both choices are very good, but they are for different types of people. University is great for students who love reading, writing, and doing research. Vocational training is perfect for students who like active work and want to learn a specific job quickly.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does university usually take to complete?
questions.explanation: The passage says: 'It usually takes three to four years to complete.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do university students do during their study?
questions.explanation: The text states that university students 'listen to lectures, read many books, and write long essays.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does vocational training usually take?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'It usually takes only one or two years.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students do at a vocational school?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Instead, they learn by doing.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University is often called higher education.
questions.explanation: The second paragraph starts with: 'University is often called higher education.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational training is longer than university.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'This training is much shorter than university.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students at vocational schools learn how to fix cars.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'For example, they learn how to fix cars, cook food, build houses, or repair computers.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational training is completely free for everyone.
questions.explanation: The text mentions that vocational training is 'often cheaper than university', but it does not say it is completely free for everyone.

questions.explanation: The last paragraph says: 'University is great for students who love reading, writing, and doing research.'

questions.explanation: The text says: 'People go to university because they want to get a college degree. This degree is important for jobs like teachers, doctors...'

questions.text: Where do vocational students practice real work with their hands?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'They practice real work with their hands in workshops.'

questions.text: What do vocational school students want to get quickly?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Students at vocational schools want to get a job quickly.'

vocabulary.meaning: Education and skills training for a specific job or trade.
Education at a college or university level.
A qualification given to a student who finishes university.
Rooms or buildings where people use tools and machines to make or repair things.
Spending money or time to get a good result in the future.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Dạng bài Multiple Choice: Đọc kỹ từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'how long', 'what do'). Tìm các con số hoặc động từ hành động trực tiếp trong bài đọc. Ở trình độ A1-A2, đáp án thường xuất hiện giống hệt từ vựng trong bài đọc.

2. Dạng bài True/False/Not Given: Đối chiếu từng từ trong câu hỏi với câu văn trong bài. Nếu thông tin trái ngược hoàn toàn (ví dụ: 'longer' vs 'shorter'), chọn FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc tới (ví dụ: 'completely free'), chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Dạng bài Short-answer: Chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Tìm từ khóa chính xác trong bài đọc và điền đúng dạng từ, không thay đổi từ của bài đọc.
